name: Gap Mangione
abstract: Gaspare Charles "Gap" Mangione ( man-JOH-nee; born July 31, 1938) is a jazz pianist from Rochester, New York. He is the brother of Chuck Mangione.

interviews.title: Gap Mangione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

concerts.label: A Chuck Mangione Concert with Concert Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Joe LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: I got through my military service fortunately and came home and went back with Gap and then went out with Woody’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe LaBarbera interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 15, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: Chuck and Gap were local musicians... I just went in and played the gig. And we became friends. Actually I had known Chuck for a long time before I went into the service. But I started working that gig and we became friends.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Lateano
about_quotes.evidence: we remained friends and whenever he came through town... he was here with Gap Mangione in a little band... And we’d always get together and hang out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Lateano interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Alkyer
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember a review by a release by Gap Mangione... And I sort of knew Gap, not at the time, but I knew Chuck.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Alkyer interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 10, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: He worked with Gap Mangione, Chuck’s older brother you know, playing the piano and Gap, playing the piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Donn Andre
about_quotes.evidence: So we played at Elmira College... there was a nice little trio of Carter, Mangione and Jimmy Wormworth.
about_quotes.doc_title: Donn Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 6, 2009
